incident_name: Marks & Spencer cyberattack — provenance probe A (SYNTHETIC)
_probe_note: SYNTHETIC PROVENANCE-VARIANT FIXTURE. The provenance attached to each claim here is deliberately counterfactual and is NOT a statement about the real public record. Its only purpose is to verify the engine tiers a claim from its SUPPLIED provenance, not from latent knowledge of the M&S incident. See fixtures/GROUND_TRUTH.md §Provenance-variant pair. In variant A the provenance of the two claims is the inverse of variant B.

incident_sources.label: [PROBE] Specialist press, unnamed sources — help-desk reset claim
incident_sources.incentive_bias: specialist press primary — single chain of unnamed sources
incident_sources.text: Sources familiar with the matter told reporters that the attackers gained initial access by socially engineering the IT help desk into resetting an internal user's password (T1566.004 / T1656). This has not been officially confirmed.

incident_sources.label: [PROBE — COUNTERFACTUAL] National regulator advisory — NTDS.dit claim
incident_sources.incentive_bias: regulator — authoritative; floor not full picture
incident_sources.text: A national cyber-security regulator advisory states, as a confirmed finding of its investigation, that the attackers obtained a copy of the NTDS.dit Active Directory database during the intrusion.
